steps.title: Identify the defining context
steps.content: Trofimov says he has “undergone a great deal” and immediately describes suffering, hunger, illness, and displacement — all experiential hardships. The word is tied to the tangible consequences of life events, not abstract discovery, neglect, or communication.

steps.title: Match the word to the context
steps.content: The target meaning is experienced something difficult. Choice C captures this by linking “undergone” directly to lived experiences, as in “gone through trials.” Choices A (“discovered”), B (“dismissed”), and D (“communicated”) either reverse the relationship or describe unrelated actions. That matches choice C — C is correct.
